ucs.title: [LLM] Kimsuky (APT43) spearphish chain: Office/HWP spawning mshta/PowerShell to fetch staged HTA from compromised infrastructure
ucs.description: Hunts Kimsuky's signature initial-access-to-execution chain where a Korean-targeted spearphishing lure (Office doc, HWP, or LNK delivered via Naver/Daum/Kakao-themed credential bait) drops or invokes mshta.exe / wscript / powershell.exe that beacons to a compromised website hosting a second-stage HTA or VBS (BabyShark, AppleSeed, ReconShark, GoldDragon loaders).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_name) as child_process values(Processes.process_command_line) as cmdline values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","hwp.exe","hword.exe","hcell.exe","gswin64c.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("mshta.exe","powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process_command_line Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(cmdline,"(?i)(http[s]?://|\\.hta|FromBase64String|DownloadString|IEX|Invoke-Expression|-enc |-EncodedCommand|bitsadmin|certutil|mshta|naver|daum|kakao|hancom)") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let officeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","hwp.exe","hword.exe","hcell.exe","gswin64c.exe"]);
let suspChildren = dynamic(["mshta.exe","powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (officeParents)
| where FileName in~ (suspChildren)
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(https?://|\.hta|FromBase64String|DownloadString|IEX|Invoke-Expression|-enc |-EncodedCommand|bitsadmin|certutil)"
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any("naver","daum","kakao","hancom","unification","mofa.go.kr")
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (suspChildren)
    | where RemoteUrl matches regex @"(?i)\.(hta|vbs|ps1|jse)$" or RemotePort in (80,443)
    | project DeviceId, NetTime=Timestamp, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, InitiatingProcessId
) on DeviceId, $left.ProcessId == $right.InitiatingProcessId
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName=AccountUpn, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Kimsuky/APT43/Emerald Sleet's documented playbook (CISA AA20-301A, Mandiant APT43 report, KISA reporting) consistently chains a weaponized Office/HWP attachment or LNK to mshta or PowerShell that pulls a staged HTA/VBS payload (BabyShark, AppleSeed, FlowerPower, GoldDragon, ReconShark). The HWP (Hancom) parent process and Korean-portal lure strings (Naver, Daum, MOFA, unification ministry) are nea

ucs.title: [LLM] Kimsuky/TA427 credential theft via fake Naver/Daum/Google portal + Gmail forwarding-rule persistence
ucs.description: Detects Kimsuky's hallmark post-compromise step on cloud identities: after harvesting M365/Gmail creds via a typosquatted Naver/Daum/Google login page (or AiTM via EvilProxy-style kit), the operator signs in from KP-adjacent infra (often CN/RU/VPS) and immediately creates an inbox forwarding/redirect rule to exfiltrate diplomatic and academic correspondence (T1114.003).
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Authentication.src) as src_ip values(Authentication.app) as app from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action=success Authentication.signature_id IN ("UserLoggedIn","UserLoginSuccess") by Authentication.user Authentication.src Authentication.app _time span=1h
| `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)`
| iplocation src
| search Country IN ("China","Russia","Korea, Democratic People's Republic of","Hong Kong","Japan","Singapore") OR match(src,"(?i)(m247|stark-industries|hostwinds|leaseweb|choopa|vultr|datacamp)")
| join type=inner user [
    search index=o365 (Operation IN ("New-InboxRule","Set-InboxRule","UpdateInboxRules","Set-Mailbox") OR Operation="Set-MailboxAutoReplyConfiguration")
    | rex field=Parameters "ForwardTo\":\"(?<fwd_addr>[^\"]+)\""
    | rex field=Parameters "(?i)(DeleteMessage|MoveToFolder|MarkAsRead)\":(?<stealth>true)"
    | where isnotnull(fwd_addr) OR stealth="true"
    | rename UserId as user
    | table _time user Operation fwd_addr stealth ClientIP
]
| where abs(_time - strptime(_time,"%s")) < 7200
| table _time user src Country app Operation fwd_addr stealth ClientIP
ucs.kql:
let suspSignins =
    AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ErrorCode == 0
    | where Country in ("CN","RU","KP","HK","JP","SG") or ISP has_any("M247","Stark","Hostwinds","Leaseweb","Choopa","Vultr","Datacamp","PONYNET")
    | project SigninTime=Timestamp, AccountUpn, IPAddress, Country, ISP, UserAgent;
let rules =
    CloudAppEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("New-InboxRule","Set-InboxRule","UpdateInboxRules","Set-Mailbox")
    | extend Params = tostring(RawEventData.Parameters)
    | where Params has_any("ForwardTo","RedirectTo","ForwardingSmtpAddress","DeleteMessage","MoveToFolder")
    | extend ForwardAddr = extract(@"(?i)(?:ForwardTo|RedirectTo|ForwardingSmtpAddress)[^A-Za-z0-9]+([^\"\}\]]+)", 1, Params)
    | project RuleTime=Timestamp, AccountUpn=AccountId, ActionType, Params, ForwardAddr, ClientIP=IPAddress;
suspSignins
| join kind=inner rules on AccountUpn
| where datetime_diff('minute', RuleTime, SigninTime) between (0 .. 240)
| where isnotempty(ForwardAddr)
   and not(ForwardAddr endswith "@yourcorp.com")
| project SigninTime, RuleTime, AccountUpn, IPAddress, Country, ISP, UserAgent, ActionType, ForwardAddr, Params
| order by RuleTime desc
ucs.rationale: Mandiant (APT43), Microsoft (Emerald Sleet/Thallium DCU lawsuit), Proofpoint (TA427), and PwC (Black Banshee) all document Kimsuky's post-credential-theft pattern of immediately writing a hidden forwarding rule on the victim mailbox to siphon foreign-policy, NK-defector, think-tank, and academic correspondence — frequently from infrastructure in CN/RU bulletproof VPS providers.
